element_introduction_order.mastery_context: Tutorial-level engagement with core run-and-gun mechanics
Early exposure to thematic enemy variety (creatures from horror exhibit)
Narrative hub: saving town from horror exhibit across five themed worlds

rhythm_pattern.pacing_descriptor: Arcade-driven tension with 360-degree combat creating constant spatial threat awareness; pacing likely escalates across five-world structure with horror-themed intensity variations

difficulty_curve.early_game: Foundational run-and-gun mechanics introduced with manageable enemy pressure
notable_spikes: World transitions introduce new thematic enemy types

notable_level_patterns.description: Five distinct worlds, each themed around horror exhibit attractions; progression tied to saving town from supernatural threats
Run-and-gun gameplay emphasizing omnidirectional shooting action, distinguishing it from horizontal-axis predecessors
Levels support both single-player and co-op modes with shared space navigation

sources_used: wikipedia - Ghoul Patrol main article (gameplay, development, release, reception)
tcrf - The Cutting Room Floor (developer credits, sequel information)
